content.text: What is the latest news about Azure AI today?

queries: latest news about Azure AI today
action.query: latest news about Azure AI today

content.text:
Here’s a comprehensive, up-to-date briefing on the **latest Azure AI news as of today**, Thursday, May 28, 2026. I’ve structured this analysis into clear sections—with citations for every key point—to give you full transparency into the sources and publication dates.

---

Development Highlights Today (May 28, 2026)
---------------------------------------------

• **General Availability (GA) of new services and features**  
  - *MySQL Flexible Server Quota Management Self-Service Experience* is now generally available in Azure, enabling customers to manage quotas themselves in the MySQL Flexible Server environment ([azurecharts.com](https://azurecharts.com/updates)).  
  - *Application Gateway for Containers* now integrates with Istio service mesh, released as GA today ([azurecharts.com](https://azurecharts.com/updates)).  
  - *Access Your SRE Agent from Any IDE, Terminal, or AI Assistant* has been announced as a notice, enabling improved accessibility to site reliability engineering agents in different developer environments ([azurecharts.com](https://azurecharts.com/updates)).

 These updates, logged today (May 28, 2026), emphasize Microsoft’s continued push to enhance infrastructure flexibility and developer tools integration across its AI-enabled cloud services.

---

Recent Azure AI and Related Initiatives (Past Few Weeks)
---------------------------------------------------------

**May 27–May 14, 2026 Updates:**  
- Azure Cosmos DB featured a blog post titled *“I’m Starting a New Cosmos DB App”*, posted May 27 ([azurecharts.com](https://azurecharts.com/updates)).  
- Significant partnerships and enterprise-scale initiatives have emerged:  

  1. **EY‑Microsoft Global Initiative**  
     On May 21, EY and Microsoft announced a $1 billion-plus, five-year strategic initiative to scale AI across organizations with enterprise-level outcomes. This includes deploying Copilot across EY’s 400,000 employees and leveraging “forward deployed engineers” to accelerate business AI transformation ([ey.com](https://www.ey.com/en_gl/newsroom/2026/05/ey-and-microsoft-announce-global-initiative-to-help-clients-scale-ai-enterprise-wide-value-creation-and-move-beyond-experimentation)).

  2. **Microsoft AI Spending & Infrastructure Expansion**  
     A detailed report estimated Microsoft’s AI-related capital expenditure run-rate at approximately $150 billion annually as of early 2026—fueled by accelerated Azure expansion and infrastructure growth. Despite surging AI revenue (Azure cloud growth of 39%, AI making up 13–16 percentage points), Microsoft faces challenges with Copilot adoption, which remains low against a large installed base ([tech-insider.org](https://tech-insider.org/microsoft-ai-spending-azure-copilot-2026/)).

  3. **Copilot Adoption Momentum**  
     An analyst from BNP Paribas highlighted robust Azure AI momentum, projecting Copilot user seats topping 25 million and Azure growth above 40 percent ([msn.com](https://www.msn.com/en-us/money/general/microsoft-s-ai-momentum-builds-analyst-sees-copilot-seats-topping-25-million-azure-growth-above-40/ar-AA247DeW)).

  4. **Copilot Agents & Partner Opportunities**  
     Microsoft Commercial CEO Judson Althoff emphasized the opportunity AI agents and Copilot present for partners in delivering managed services ([crn.com](https://www.crn.com/news/cover-story/microsoft-s-judson-althoff-to-partners-managed-services-are-your-ai-superpower)).

  5. **Denodo Agora Service at Microsoft Marketplace**  
     Denodo launched Agora, a managed platform with native integration into Microsoft services such as Fabric, now available via the Marketplace to support agentic AI scenarios ([markets.businessinsider.com](https://markets.businessinsider.com/news/stocks/agora-the-denodo-cloud-service-now-available-on-the-microsoft-marketplace-to-power-agentic-ai-use-cases-1036197653)).

**Broader Azure Feature Updates (Mid to Late May):**  
- **Azure Weekly Newsletter (Issue #564, May 24):**  
  Highlighted enhancements in Microsoft Foundry—AI agents, memory stores, human-in-the-loop patterns, and integrations with models like Tongyi‑MAI Z‑Image‑Turbo, Flux.1‑schnell, SDXL base 1.0, and Cohere Command A+ ([azureweekly.info](https://azureweekly.info/latest/)).  
- **Azure Update Summary (May 22):**  
  Focused on deeper service integration, automation, and enterprise-grade security, covering AKS auto instrumentation, AppConfig scorecards, TLS deprecation, insights for Cosmos AI, and Front Door updates ([hubsite365.com](https://www.hubsite365.com/en-ww/crm-pages/azure-update-22nd-may-2026.htm)).  
- **Azure Infrastructure Enhancements (Weeks 19–20):**  
  Azure IaaS & Azure Local saw manifold improvements across networking, storage, and datacenter management portfolios, reflecting Microsoft’s responsive cloud evolution ([francescomolfese.it](https://francescomolfese.it/2026/05/azure-iaas-and-azure-local-announcements-and-updates-2026-weeks-19-20/)).

---

Broader AI and Azure Strategy Context
---------------------------------------

• **Microsoft‑OpenAI Partnership Update (April 27, 2026):**  
  The amended agreement broadens Microsoft’s rights to OpenAI’s IP through 2032, makes OpenAI free to serve products across cloud providers, and shifts to a non‑exclusive model—though with continued first-launch preference on Azure ([openai.com](https://openai.com/index/next-phase-of-microsoft-partnership/)).

• **Anthropic & NVIDIA Strategic Alignment:**  
  Though announced in late 2025, this partnership remains highly relevant: Anthropic’s Claude models are scaled on Azure with NVIDIA compute; customers via Microsoft Foundry can access Claude frontier models (Sonnet 4.5, Opus 4.1, Haiku 4.5); and investment commitments from Microsoft and NVIDIA total up to $15 billion ([anthropic.com](https://www.anthropic.com/news/microsoft-nvidia-anthropic-announce-strategic-partnerships)).

• **Maia AI Chip Availability:**  
  Reports indicate that Microsoft’s second-generation Maia AI chip (announced January) is still not yet available through Azure, though it is intended to support OpenAI’s GPT‑5.2 workloads ([cnbc.com](https://www.cnbc.com/2026/05/21/anthropic-microsoft-maia-200-ai-chip.html)).

---

Summary of Azure AI’s Current Momentum (May 28, 2026)
-------------------------------------------------------

1. **Today’s Major Release Highlights**  
   - GA rollout for MySQL Flexible Server quota self‑service and Application Gateway with Istio integration.  
   - New capabilities for accessing SRE agents across development environments.

2. **Momentum in AI Infrastructure & Business Expansion**  
   - Massive AI infrastructure investments (~$150 billion/year run-rate), fueling Azure growth.  
   - Strategic partnership with EY to scale AI enterprise-wide.

3. **Copilot Ecosystem**  
   - Rising adoption forecast (25 million seats), though adoption still lags against installed base.  
   - AI agents framing new partner service strategies.

4. **Platform & Model Reach Broadens**  
   - Denodo’s Agora enriches agentic AI workflows via Marketplace.  
   - Foundry enhancements deepen support for diverse AI models and agent constructs.

5. **Ecosystem Strategy & Partnerships**  
   - OpenAI deals restructure to provide long-term flexibility.  
   - Claude via Anthropic on Azure enhances model diversity.  
   - Maia chip pending availability in Azure affects compute backend.

---

Recommended Next Actions for You
---------------------------------

- **Explore the Today-GA Features**: If you use MySQL Flexible Server or Application Gateway for containerized workloads, evaluate how the new GA features can streamline your operations.
- **Leverage Partner-Led AI Strategies**: If you’re involved in consulting or enterprise IT, consider engaging with or watching EY’s approach to scaling Copilot and AI transformation.
- **Integrate with Azure Foundry and Agora**: For teams building AI agents or leveraging data integrations, the enhanced Foundry Labs capabilities and Denodo’s Marketplace integration can accelerate development.
- **Track Platform Infrastructure Upgrades**: Keep tabs on when the Maia 200 chip becomes accessible on Azure to align compute-intensive AI workloads.

---

In total, these developments amount to a robust expansion of Azure AI's capabilities across infrastructure, tooling, enterprise adoption, and platform strategy—anchored by today’s significant infrastructure releases and bolstered by adjacent strategic partnerships.

Let me know if you'd like to dive deeper into any specific area—such as technical details of Azure Foundry improvements, Copilot adoption analytics, or enterprise migration options!
